title: Clinical Significance of Telomerase Reverse-Transcriptase Promoter Mutations in Hepatocellular Carcinoma
author: Pezzuto, Francesca
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Activating mutations in the promoter region of TERT (TERTp) gene are frequently observed in low- and high-grade dysplastic nodules and defined as early events in hepatocellular carcinoma development. This study shows that the nucleotide change G>A at position −124 in the TERTp region is very common in hepatocellular carcinoma. The concordance rate between droplet digital PCR (ddPCR) (63.6%) and Sanger sequencing (52.1%) detection methods is good (83.5%). HCC patients carrying the TERTp mutation had lower levels of the tumour biomarker Ca19-9 but showed reduced survival. The presence of TERTp mutations may represent a prognostic signature in liver cancer.

The presence of TERTp mutations may represent a prognostic signature in liver cancer.
keywords: TERT promoter; TERTp; hepatocellular carcinoma; HCC; droplet digital PCR; ddPCR; hepatitis B virus; HBV; hepatitis C virus; HCV; cirrhosis; mutation
